mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS still near zero (0.38%) while CPIAUCSL shows persistent inflation, a liftoff in the policy rate of more than 0.25pp within 3 months signals the start of an aggressive tightening cycle that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the following month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.25pp within a 3-month window from a near-zero base
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.38% as of 2021-12 signals pre-liftoff stance ahead of anticipated 2022 hikes
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following liftoff triggers are positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A monotonic 3-month decline in UNRATE reflects tightening labor markets and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% continuing a declining trend from pandemic peak
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month UNRATE trend and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in HOUST greater than 5% signals a slowdown in residential construction demand, a leading indicator of broader economic deceleration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as growth expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: HOUST year-over-year percent change falls below -5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1164k units as of 2021-11 near cycle highs, vulnerable to reversal amid rising rate expectations
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with rising rate expectations
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST decline triggers are positive in more than 60% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO's 3-month moving average rises while RSAFS's 3-month moving average falls, production is outpacing consumer demand, indicating unintended inventory buildup that foreshadows margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month MA rising while RSAFS 3-month MA falling simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.13 and RSAFS at 456978 million as of latest reading show divergent supply-demand momentum entering 2022
failure_condition: Divergence-triggered instances show 20-day forward returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS begins rising from a near-zero base while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 5%, the joint signal of policy tightening under high inflation forces markets to price a faster hiking path, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as valuation multiples contract.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS begins rising while CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 5%
evidence_refs: fact:high inflation environment coinciding with anticipated 2022 Fed liftoff from 0.38% policy rate
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle onset with above-target inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns in joint-trigger periods are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus single-series FEDFUNDS signal.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth below 1% signals weakening labor demand momentum ahead of visible layoffs, prompting downward revisions to earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth rate falls below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144175 thousand as of latest reading, recovering but growth pace closely watched for deceleration signals
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following PAYEMS deceleration triggers are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending, a primary driver of corporate revenue, and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week as near-term earnings sentiment improves.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 456978 million as of latest month reflecting continued consumer spending strength
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion when UNRATE is near cyclical lows (below 5%), a deceleration in INDPRO year-over-year growth signals capacity constraints and slowing output that foreshadow earnings deceleration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth decelerates while UNRATE remains below 5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% and INDPRO at 104.13 indicate tight labor market alongside moderating industrial output growth
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during joint low-unemployment/decelerating-INDPRO regimes are positive in more than 55% of instances, or interaction Rank IC does not exceed single-series INDPRO Rank IC by at least 0.02.
